description_long: This object is a rectangular stone pedestal constructed from large, roughly hewn blocks with visible mortar lines and pronounced surface cracks. The pedestal has a wider top and base, tapering slightly in the middle, giving it a classic architectural look. The stone blocks are a warm, earthy brown with a pale gray border at the top and bottom, suggesting a capstone and base. The surface texture is rough and weathered, with irregularities and chips that add to its aged appearance. The block arrangement is regular, but the cracks and surface wear indicate long-term exposure to the elements. This type of structure is commonly used as a decorative or functional barrier in urban parks, plazas, or historical sites.
description: A weathered, rectangular stone pedestal made of large brown blocks with a gray cap and base, featuring visible cracks and rough texture. It has a classic, slightly tapered form and an aged appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full face of the pedestal with prominent cracks and a clear outline of the capstone and base.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, with similar block arrangement and surface cracks, but minor differences in crack patterns.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirrors the previous sides, maintaining the same block pattern and weathered details.
description_view_3: Back view is visually indistinguishable from the other sides, with matching block layout and surface wear.
attribute_location_context: urban park or plaza as a decorative or functional barrier
attribute_installation_method: mortared in place on a solid foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Visible surface cracks and chipped edges suggest long-term weathering and exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated, likely used as a standalone pedestal or barrier
